title: Adobo Black Bean Tostadas With Shredded Lettuce, Lime Cashew Crema, Avocado & Salsa Macha
method:
Adobo Black Bean Tostadas With Shredded Lettuce, Lime Cashew Crema, Avocado & Salsa MachaOur smokey, slow-cooked black beans are layered with a multitude of complex flavours like cumin, paprika and ancho chillies, served on crispy corn tostadas and topped with crisp shredded lettuce and creamy avocado. Finished with a zingy lime cashew crema and our house made salsa macha featuring crushed peanuts and three types of Mexican chillies.Prep time2Total time10Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Take out a saucepan and preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan.STEP 2Traditionally tostadas are shallow fried in oil for 30 seconds on each side, which you can do if you want them to turn really crispy. However for our healthier, oil-free version, simply pop thetacosonto a baking tray with space in between them and bake for 8 minutes or until crispy, flipping half way.STEP 3Drain theblackbeansand empty into your saucepan along with thesmoky adobo sauce, place over a medium heat and warm through, stirring occasionally, until piping hot, around 5 minutes. Add a splash of water if it starts to stick at the bottom. Roughly mash around half of the beans with a fork and set aside.STEP 4Finely shred thebabygemby cutting horizontally across the head. De-stone theavocadoand cut into thin slices flat side down. If you're feeling fancy you could fan the avocado for a more elevated final presentation. Using the palm of your hand, carefully push the avocado down and away from yourself to open it up like a fan without the slices breaking. Cut thelimeinto wedges.STEP 5Lay everything out on your table and then start building your tostadas. Start with a layer ofblackbeans, then shreddedlettuce, dress the lettuce with a drizzle ofcashewcrema, top with some slices ofavocadoand a final drizzle ofsalsamacha. Finish with a squeeze of freshlimejuice, and enjoy!What’s in your boxSmoky Adobo SauceBlack BeansSalsa MachaFreshly Baked TacosCashew CremaBaby Gem LettuceAvocadoLimeNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories753kcalTotal fat48.4gSaturated fat8.3gSalt1.5gTotal carbohydrate64.9gTotal sugar13gFibre19.4gProtein21.3gIngredients / AllergensBlack beans, red onion, garlic, cumin, smoked sweet paprika, ground coriander, cinnamon, clove, oregano, tomato purée, raw organic agave nectar, chipotle paste, balsamic vinegar (sulphites), water, sea salt, cashews (nuts), organic cultured soy yoghurt (soy), fresh lemon juice, garlic powder, extra virgin olive oil, rapeseed oil, ancho chiles, guajillo chiles, roastedpeanuts,sesameseeds, apple cider vinegar (sulphites), corn tortillas (yellow corn flour), baby gem lettuce, lime, avocadoFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Afghan Aubergine (Borani Banjan) With Saucy Spiced Lentils, Garlic Yoghurt, Fresh Mint & Pillowy Flatbreads
method:
Afghan Aubergine (Borani Banjan) With Saucy Spiced Lentils, Garlic Yoghurt, Fresh Mint & Pillowy FlatbreadsThis dish is inspired by the Afghan recipe, borani banjan, which made with aubergine and yogurt. Crispy roasted aubergine sit over a rich tomato sauce infused with garlic and spices. Although not traditional, we've added lentils to the sauce for extra protein, iron and fibre for gut health. It's all finished with garlic yoghurt, fresh mint and served with our pillowy soft garlic and herb flatbreads for mopping everything up. It's a delicious dish to put in the centre of the table and enjoy feasting style.Prep time1Total time20Cooking Mode:

title: Aromatic Thai Green Curry Soup With Beluga Lentils, Tender Aubergine, Sweet Potato, Basil & Lime
method:
Aromatic Thai Green Curry Soup With Beluga Lentils, Tender Aubergine, Sweet Potato, Basil & LimeOur super green Thai curry soup is infused with punchy aromatic flavours like kaffir lime leaves, lemongrass, galangal and garlic with a few subtle spices, all simmered in decadent coconut milk and finished with fresh basil. At home you'll fill it with roasted aubergine and sweet potato which are both full of vitamins B, C and antioxidants. We've also swapped rice for tender beluga lentils which is great for your gut and is high in protein.Prep timeTotal timeCooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large roasting tray, and a large saucepan.STEP 2Dice thesweet potatoandaubergineinto 1.5cm cubes. Add the sweet potato to one side of your roasting tray and the aubergine to the other. Drizzle generously with olive oil, season with salt and pepper and roast until tender, around 20-30 minutes.STEP 3Once there's 5 minutes left on the vegetables, add thegreen curry soupto your saucepan. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook for a further 2-3 minutes or until piping hot.Meanwhile drain and rinse the lentils well. Roll up thebasilleaves with your hands and finely slice them. Set aside a quarter of the basil leaves for a garnish.Add the drainedlentilsand the remaining three quarters ofbasilto the soup and cook for a further 2 minutes. Remove from the heat, squeeze in half of the lime and season to taste with salt.STEP 4To serve, divide thethai green curry soupandlentilsbetween serving bowls, arrange thesweetpotatoandauberginearound the bowl and garnish with the remainingbasilleaves. Squeeze over the remaininglimeand enjoy!What’s in your boxGreen Curry SoupSweet PotatoBasilBlack LentilsAubergineLimeNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories769kcalTotal fat37.3gSaturated fat31.8gSalt1gTotal carbohydrate102.1gTotal sugar23.1gFibre24.7gProtein18.7gIngredients / AllergensSpring onion, garlic, ginger, green chilli, lemongrass, fresh coriander, ground cumin, ground turmeric, ground coriander, coconut milk, coconut cream, spinach, raw organic agave nectar, lime leaves, sea salt, sweet potato, beluga lentils, lime, aubergine, fresh basilFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Rich Ragu & Roasted Roots Gratin, Topped With Crispy Potato, Beetroot, Sweet Potato & Creamy Miso Bechamel
method:
Rich Ragu & Roasted Roots Gratin, Topped With Crispy Potato, Beetroot, Sweet Potato & Creamy Miso BechamelA rich, hearty, warming dish that's brings together the best of a gratin and a shepherds pie. Featuring a rich lentil ragu, topped with sliced potato, beetroot and sweet potato, and a final layer of creamy miso bechamel. There's minimal prep and it's all roasted in one dish so it's super simple after a long day. When it comes out of the oven the ragu is bubbling, the roasted root veg is crispy and the creamy bechamel delivers a heavenly 'cheesy' flavour.. A seriously delicious way to hit your veg, protein, fibre and iron goals.Prep timeTotal timeCooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan. Take out a large casserole dish or an ovenproof dish.STEP 2Scrub thebeetrootto remove any dirt from the skin. Then slice thebeetroot,sweet potato, andpotato, as thinly as possible, around 1.5-2 mm thick. Use a mandoline slicer if you have one. Toss the sliced vegetables in olive oil, salt and pepper in a bowl.STEP 3In your casserole or ovenproof dish, mix together theroots gratin raguand the tin ofblack lentils, including the liquid from the can. Spread evenly to cover the base. Lay the slicedbeetroot,sweet potato, andpotatoon top so they are overlapping and covering the whole dish. We recommend alternating the vegetables as the colours look really beautiful together! For example lay 3 slices of beetroot, then 3 slices of sweet potato and so on.Pour themiso bechamelevenly over the top, then cover with a lid or a sheet of aluminium foil and bake for 20 minutes, until the vegetables are tender. Remove the lid or foil and bake for a further 10 minutes, or until the root vegetables have crispy edges and the bechamel is golden in places.STEP 4To serve, divide thegratinbetween dishes. Finish with a sprinkle of salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Enjoy!What’s in your boxRoots Gratin RaguMiso BechamelBlack LentilsBeetrootSweet PotatoPotatoNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories591kcalTotal fat8.3gSaturated fat1.6gSalt1.8gTotal carbohydrate96.1gTotal sugar30.3gFibre19.2gProtein23.6gIngredients / AllergensMaris piper potato, sweet potato, beetroot, black lentils, onion, carrot,celery, garlic, thyme, rosemary, red wine (sulphites), gluten freesoysauce, balsamic vinegar (sulphites), raw organic agave nectar, marmite (yeast extract (barley, wheat, oats, rye) (gluten), salt, vegetable extract, vitamins (thiamin, riboflavin, niacin, vitamin b12, folic acid), natural flavouring (celery)), sea salt, tomato purée, tomatoes, black pepper, extra virgin olive oil, rapeseed oil,soymilk, nutritional yeast, potato flakes, garlic powder, onion powder, rice vinegar, white miso (soybeans, rice, salt), fresh lemon juiceFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Black Bean Smash Burger Tacos With Homemade Burger Sauce & Cabbage Carrot Apple Walnut Slaw
method:
Black Bean Smash Burger Tacos With Homemade Burger Sauce & Cabbage Carrot Apple Walnut SlawWe have perfected the ultimate smash burger recipe - meaty, juicy, umami-rich - which works perfectly in a crispy folded taco, filled with a fresh, nutrient rich slaw of red cabbage, apple, carrot and walnut, and a house made 'burger sauce'. Tastes like a cheat meal but is the total opposite - high protein, high fibre and full of whole food ingredients like vegetables, beans, nuts and seeds.Prep time8Total time10Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Take out a large frying pan and two mixing bowls.STEP 2To prepare the slaw, cut and remove the core of theapple. Top and tail thecarrot(peel if you prefer), and remove the hard core of thered cabbage. Julienne all of them into uniform, thin shreds (use a mandoline if you have one).Place them in one of the mixing bowls and season with the juice of half alime, a drizzle of olive oil, and salt and pepper to taste. Roughly chop thewalnuts, fold them in, and set aside.Cut the remaining lime into wedges for garnish and set aside.STEP 3To prepare the burger mixture, place your frying pan over medium-low heat and dry toast (without oil) thegram flourfor 2 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it becomes golden.Drain theblack beansand transfer them into the other mixing bowl. Roughly mash them with the back of a fork. Add the toasted gram flour and thepatty flavour bomb, then stir well to combine.STEP 4Wipe clean the frying pan you used for toasting the gram flour and place it over medium heat and add a drizzle of olive oil.While the oil heats up, divide the burger mixture and spread it on each of theflour tortillas. Place them on the pan with the burger mixture side down and press with a spatula and cook for 4-5 minutes until the mixture starts to caramelise. Flip it onto the other side and cook further 1minute until the tortilla is golden. Cook in a few batches depending on the size of your frying pan, adding more oil for each batch.STEP 5To Serve, add a dollop of theburger sauceon each of the burger tortillas and fold them in half. Plate them up along with theslawandlimewedges. Enjoy!What’s in your boxPatty Flavour BombBlack BeansGram FlourBurger SauceFlour TortillaRed CabbageCarrotWalnutsAppleLimeNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories735kcalTotal fat32.3gSaturated fat4.1gSalt3.2gTotal carbohydrate86.2gTotal sugar21.8gFibre21gProtein26.9gIngredients / AllergensMushroom, onion, chickpea flour, miso, (soy), balsamic vinegar, gluten free low sodium soy sauce, garlic, paprika, oregano, tomato puree, raw organic agave nectar, water, sea salt, black beans, flour tortillas (wheat flour (gluten), calcium carbonate, calcium propionate, iron, niacin, thiamin), red cabbage, carrot, walnut (nuts), apple, lime,soymilk, rice vinegar, rapeseed oil, ground onion, ground garlic, cornichons, dijonmustardFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Crispy Sprouts & Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin Seeds
method:
Butternut Squash Brothy Beans With Crispy Sprouts & Sage, Hot Agave 'Nduja' Dressing, Toasted Pine Nuts & Pumpkin SeedsThis might just be the best recipes we've tasted coming out of our test kitchen. The broth is the star of the show, featuring a rich onion, garlic and white wine broth, stirred through with sweet and nutty pureed butternut squash. At home you'll simmer tender butterbeans in the delicious broth, then top with crispy roasted sprouts, a drizzle of hot agave 'nduja' oil to cut through the richness and a sprinkle of toasted pine nuts and pumpkin seeds for a final hit of nutrition and flavour. 20g of fibre and 20g of protein make this dish as nutritious as it is delicious.Prep time5Total time20Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Preheat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a large saucepan, a frying pan and a baking tray.STEP 2Trim the bottoms of thebrusselssproutsif needed, then slice them thinly with a sharp knife. Alternatively you can cut them in half but thinly slicing will make them crispier and quicker to cook. Place the sprouts onto your baking tray, toss in a little olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Roast in the oven until brown and crisp, around 15-20 minutes, turning/tossing halfway.STEP 3Drain thebutterbeansand add to your saucepan along with thesquash broth. Once there is 5 minutes left of cooking time on the sprouts, place the pan over a medium-high heat. Once the sauce is bubbling, reduce to low-medium and cook for 2-3 minutes or until piping hot.STEP 4Place your frying pan over a low-medium heat, then dry fry (without oil) thepumpkin and pine nutsuntil lightly toasted. Remove from the pan and set aside. Add a few tablespoons of olive oil to the pan and place it back over a medium heat. When hot, add thesageleaves without overlapping. The leaves will turn translucent and the oil will stop bubbling around the leaves when they are crisp. Remove and set aside.STEP 5To serve up, divide thebutternut squash brothy beansbetween serving bowls and top with the crispy shreddedsprouts, toastedpumpkin and pine nuts, crispysageand finish with a drizzle ofhot agave nduja. Enjoy!What’s in your boxSquash BrothBrussels SproutsPumpkin & Pine NutsHot Agave NdujaSageButterbeansNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories550kcalTotal fat22gSaturated fat5.4gSalt2gTotal carbohydrate65.5gTotal sugar13.6gFibre20gProtein21.5gIngredients / AllergensButternut squash, brussels sprouts, butter beans, sage, pumpkin seeds, pinenuts, garlic, onion, white wine (alcohol removed during cooking) (sulphites), rapeseed oil, water, kidney beans, roasted red peppers, sundried tomatoes, hot smoked paprika, sweet smoked paprika, red chilli, red chilli flakes, gochugaru, extra virgin olive oil, white miso(soybeans, rice, salt), raw organic agave nectar, lemon juiceFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Smoky Butternut Squash & "Nduja" Lasagne With Tender Lentils & Creamy Miso Spinach Bechamel
method:
Smoky Butternut Squash & "Nduja" Lasagne With Tender Lentils & Creamy Miso Spinach BechamelOur healthy spin on a lasagne, packed with 35g of protein and 30g of fibre. Featuring layers of smoky squash and 'nduja' lentils, and silky miso bechamel with baby spinach. The sauces are all made by our lovely chefs for you, so all you need to do is assemble and whack it in the oven!Prep time5Total time30Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsCooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Preheat your oven to 200°C / 180°C fan. Take out a saucepan, a mixing bowl and a baking dish around 15x20cm (or double this for a four person box).STEP 2Drain thelentilsand add them to your mixing bowl along with thesquash & nduja filling. Give everything a good mix to combine.STEP 3Place a saucepan over medium heat and add thecreamy miso bechamel. Bring it to a gentle simmer, then add thebaby spinachand cook for about a minute until wilted.STEP 4Build your lasagne in three layers:Spread one-third of thelentils, squash & nduja mixin the base of your dish. Cover withlasagne sheets(about 2½ sheets per layer).Top with one-third of thebaby spinach & miso bechamel, then another third of thelentils, squash & nduja mix.Repeat with morelasagne sheets, another third ofbaby spinach & miso bechamel, and the remaininglentils, squash & nduja mix.Finish with a final layer oflasagne sheetsand the rest of thebaby spinach & miso bechamel.Drizzle with olive oil, season with salt and pepper, then bake for 25–30 minutes, until bubbling and golden. To check doneness, insert a knife into the centre, and if it’s hot through and the pasta is tender, it's ready.STEP 5Place the lasagne in the middle of the table and divide it between serving plates. We like stacking pieces on top of each other to make one bigger slice per person, but you could serve them as they are too. Enjoy!What’s in your boxSquash & Nduja FillingMiso BechamelBaby SpinachLasagne SheetsOrganic Brown LentilsNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories664kcalTotal fat7gSaturated fat1.4gSalt1.7gTotal carbohydrate119gTotal sugar18.6gFibre29gProtein35.4gIngredients / AllergensButternut squash, water, garlic, tomato purée, smoked sweet paprika, hot smoked paprika, white miso (soybeans, rice, salt), tomatoes, raw organic agave nectar, sea salt, gluten freesoysauce, onion powder, brown lentils, extra virgin olive oil, rapeseed oil, soy milk (soybeans, water), nutritional yeast, potato flakes, garlic powder, rice vinegar, fresh lemon juice, lasagne sheets (gluten), baby spinachFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Butternut Squash & Spelt Risotto With Crispy Sage & Smoky Toasted Pine Nuts
method:
Butternut Squash & Spelt Risotto With Crispy Sage & Smoky Toasted Pine NutsThis autumnal squash dish is inspired by risotto, but made so much healthier by swapping out the traditional arborio rice for the nutritionally superior spelt. It’s a nutty, chewy grain that works so well with the super creamy miso butternut squash sauce. Finished off with a smoky pine nut drizzle and crispy sage.Prep timeTotal timeCooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Boil your kettle, then take out a large saucepan, small saucepan and frying pan.STEP 2Pour boiling water from your kettle into a large saucepan, salt the water, then place over a medium-high heat. Once the water is bubbling, add thespeltand turn it down to a simmer. Cook until al dente, around 15-20 minutes. Reserve a mug of water for later, then drain the spelt once cooked.STEP 3Once there's 5 minutes left of cooking time on your spelt, heat thebutternutsquashsaucein your other saucepan for around 3-5 minutes or until piping hot. Mix the spelt into the warmed sauce once cooked. If the sauce has reduced and needs loosening a little, and add your reserved cooking water until you have a creamy risotto consistency.STEP 4Heat a few tablespoons of olive oil in your frying pan. When hot, add thesageleaves without overlapping. The leaves will turn translucent and the oil will stop bubbling around the leaves when they are crisp. Turn off the heat and sprinkle in thesmoky pine nut mix.STEP 5To serve, divide therisottointo serving bowls, scatter over thesage,smokypinenutsand drizzle over any remaining smoky sage oil (optional). Season with salt and pepper to taste and enjoy!What’s in your boxButternut Squash SauceSmoky Pine Nut MixCavolo NeroSageSpeltNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories650kcalsTotal fat16.3gSaturated fat2.8gSalt2.81gTotal carbohydrate106.6gTotal sugarFibre15.8gProtein22.9gIngredients / AllergensOrganic pearled spelt (wheat), butternut squash, garlic, water,  white miso (soy,rice, salt, yeast, koji culture), lemon juice, preserved lemon paste, nutritional yeast, oat milk (water, oats, vitamins (D2, B12, Riboflavin, Folic Acid) (wheat), white wine (sulphites), pinenuts, smoked paprika, sea salt, fresh sageFor allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

title: Peanut Butter Ramen With Smoky Garlic & Sesame Tempeh, Pak Choi, Toasted Peanuts & Lime
method:
Peanut Butter Ramen With Smoky Garlic & Sesame Tempeh, Pak Choi, Toasted Peanuts & LimeThis one's for the ramen lovers. We've taken everything you know and love and taken it to a whole new level. Featuring a rich, moreish ramen broth that's simmered with coconut milk and peanut butter, and spiked with gochujang, a Korean fermented chilli paste that's full of flavour and great for your gut. At home you'll top it with smokey garlic and sesame tempeh mince, then nestle in noodles and greens for the ultimate healthy feast.Prep time3Total time15Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Boil your kettle, then take out a bowl, a large frying pan and a large saucepan.STEP 2Place your frying pan over medium heat and dry-toast thepeanutswithout oil until fragrant. Roughly chop them and set aside.STEP 3Crumble thetempehinto small pieces in a bowl. Wipe clean the frying pan used for the peanuts, place it back over medium-high heat, and add a generous drizzle of cooking oil (we recommend roasted sesame oil). Add the crumbled tempeh and cook for about 6 minutes, until golden brown. Add thesmoky tempeh marinadeand stir well to coat all the pieces evenly. Continue cooking for a few more minutes, until fragrant and crispy at the edges.STEP 4Meanwhile, fill your saucepans with boiling water from the kettle and place it over medium heat. Once the water is bubbling, add theflat rice noodlesand cook for 5-6 minutes, or until al dente. Drain and rinse well under cold running water, then leave them in a colander to drain. Quickly rinse the saucepan for the next step.STEP 5Slice the base off thepak choi, separate the leaves, and wash them thoroughly. If any leaves are very large, slice them in half lengthwise. Pour thepeanut butter brothinto a clean saucepan along with 200ml of water (double the amount for a four-person portion) and bring to a boil. Add thepak choileaves, reduce the heat to a simmer, and cook until tender. Squeeze in the juice of half alimeat the end and stir well. title: Rich & Creamy Tofu Butter 'Chicken' With Baby Spinach, Garlic Naan & Toasted Almond Flakes
method:
Rich & Creamy Tofu Butter 'Chicken' With Baby Spinach, Garlic Naan & Toasted Almond FlakesButter chicken is one of the most popular Indian dishes around and for good reason, with it's comforting, moreish mildly spiced creamy tomato flavour. This is our healthy whole food plant-based version, featuring high protein tofu chunks which are oven roasted for a perfectly chewy texture, then turned through our luxuriously creamy, mildly spiced curry sauce. Served with baby spinach, toasted almonds, organic cultured yoghurt and freshly baked garlic and herb naan to mop everything up with.Prep time2Total time20Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan, then take out a baking tray and deep frying pan or wok.STEP 2Tear thetofuinto bite-sized chunks, place into your roasting tray, toss through a drizzle of olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper. Roast for 20 minutes, turning halfway. The tofu should be starting to turn brown and crispy on the outside.STEP 3Place your frying pan or wok over a medium heat and dry fry (without oil) theflakedalmondsto toast. Remove and set aside. Wipe the pan clean as you'll use it again shortly.STEP 4Once the tofu has 3 minutes left of cooking time, add thebutter 'chicken' sauceto your frying pan and place over a medium heat. Add thebabyspinach, stir it through the sauce and cook until wilted. Remove the tofu from the oven and, at the same time, place thegarlic and herb naaninto the oven to warm through. title: Rich & Creamy Tofu Tikka Masala With Blistered Green Beans, Coconut Yoghurt & Fluffy Basmati Rice
method:

Meaty chunks of tofu are marinated and roasted in a tikka spice mix  for a perfectly chewy texture, then simmered in a rich and creamy masala sauce gently spiced with garlic, ginger, cumin and cardamom. Served with blistered green beans, fluffy basmati rice and finished with a drizzle of organic coconut yoghurt.Prep time2Total time20Cooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture below) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Pre-heat your oven to 220°C / 200°C fan and take out a roasting tray, saucepan and a frying pan or a griddle pan.STEP 2Place thebasmatiriceinto one of your saucepans and cover with 2.5x its volume in water. Bring to the boil, then reduce the heat, cover and continue to cook for 8-10 minutes or until tender but al dente. Drain and set aside to keep warm.STEP 3In the meantime, tear thetofuinto bite-sized chunks with your hands, aiming for bitesized pieces about 1.5-2 cm in size. Add them to a small bowl and sprinkle in thetofutikka spice mix, a drizzle of olive oil and season well with salt and pepper. Give them a mix until the tofu is nicely coated with the spices and seasoning. Spread out on your roasting tray and roast for 20 minutes, turning halfway. The tofu should be starting to turn brown and crispy on the outside.STEP 4Once there's 10 minutes left on the tofu, place your frying pan or a griddle pan over a medium-high heat and add a drizzle of olive oil. While it heats up, trim thegreenbeansand cut them into bite-sized pieces (or leave them whole if you prefer) and add to the frying pan. Fry until they starts to char and blister, around 6-8 minutes. Season to taste with salt and pepper.STEP 5When there's a 3 minutes left on the tofu, add the tikka sauce to your saucepan and warm through over a medium heat. Just before serving, stir the roasted tofu into thetikka sauceand turn off the heat. Drizzle thecoconut yoghurtin and gently stir to create a marbled effect on the surface of the sauce.STEP 6To serve, divide thericeandtofutikkamasalabetween serving bowls and serve the blisteredgreen beansalongside. Enjoy!What’s in your boxTikka SauceOrganic TofuGreen BeansCoconut YoghurtTofu Tikka SpiceBasmati RiceNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories559kcalTotal fat18.8gSaturated fat8.7gSalt2.5gTotal carbohydrate72.2gTotal sugar9.7gFibre8.4gProtein26.1gIngredients / AllergensOrganic tofu (soybeans, nigari), smoked sweet paprika, garam masala (coriander, cinnamon, cloves, ginger, star anise, cumin, chilli, fennel, black pepper, bay leaves, sunflower oil, nutmeg, cardamom), onion powder, cumin, ground coriander, turmeric, cardamom powder, garlic, ginger, onion, tomato purée, tomatoes, coconut milk, creamed coconut, mango purée, liquid smoke, fresh lemon juice, sea salt, green beans, basmati rice, coconut yoghurt (coconut milk, coconut water, corn starch, corn fibre, potato starch, fruit pectin, vegan cultures)For allergens please see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1Take out a large saucepan, a large frying pan and a large mixing bowl.STEP 2Grate thecourgettethen place it into a sieve or perforated colander rested over a bowl or colander. Toss it in a large pinch of sea salt, set aside for 10 minutes, then squeeze and drain as much liquid as you can from the courgette. If you don't do this your fritters won't crisp up.STEP 3Add thesrilankandaalsauce, red lentils and 300ml of water (or 600ml for a 4 person box) to your saucepan and cook for 10-15 mins until the lentils are soft and tender. Stir occasionally while cooking and add a splash of water as needed if it reduces too much or starts to stick. Season to taste with salt.STEP 4Drain thesweetcornand add it into your large mixing bowl with the squeezedcourgetteandfritter dry batter. Season well with salt and pepper. You may need to add a little water to bind the batter mix. The less water you use, the crispier your fritters will be so start with 50ml and add more if needed until the mix is just starting to stick together (it will bind better once it's fried!).STEP 5Heat a drizzle of oil in your frying pan over a medium heat. Add a large spoonful of the batter mix to the pan and press down to form a 1cm thick fritter (they don’t need to be perfect circles!). Cook each side for 4-5 minutes or until golden and cooked through. Repeat with the remaining batter.STEP 6Divide thesrilankandaalbetween serving bowls, top with the spiced courgette and corn fritters, drizzle over thesoyyoghurtanddatetamarindchutney, and garnish the dish with a few pickedcorianderleaves to finish. Enjoy!What’s in your boxSri Lankan DaalOrganic Soy YoghurtDate Tamarind ChutneyCourgetteFritter Dry BatterSweetcornRed LentilsCorianderNutritional infoAll nutritional values are based on a per person scale.Calories748kcalTotal fat25gSaturated fat16.7gSalt2.1gTotal carbohydrate104.5gTotal sugar34.8gFibre20.9gProtein28.8gIngredients / AllergensRed lentils, sweetcorn, courgette, fresh coriander, garlic, ginger, onion, turmeric, cumin, brownmustardseeds, red chilli, curry leaves, coconut milk, sea salt, coconut sugar, dates, water, tamarind, gluten free flour (flour blend (rice, potato, tapioca, maize, buckwheat)), smoked sweet paprika, garlic powder, baking powder, madras curry powder (ground coriander, ground turmeric,mustardpowder, bengal gram (black chickpea), ground cumin, ground chilli, ground fenugreek, ground black pepper, garlic powder, iodised salt, ground fennel.), cinnamon, organic cultured soy yoghurt (soy)For allergens, see ingredients inboldPlease be advised that we handle the following allergens in our kitchen: CELERY, SOYA, GLUTEN, NUTS, PEANUTS, MUSTARD, SESAME SEEDS, SULPHITES

method:

Served with a side of fluffy basmati rice for a delicious, high protein, high fibre bowl that will leave you feeling satisfied and full of energy.Prep timeTotal timeCooking Mode:
Cooking instructionsBefore you start, take out all the ingredients for this recipe (see picture above) and rinse any raw grains, herbs or vegetables.STEP 1We highly recommend using a cast iron pan or griddle to char the oyster mushrooms if you have one. If you don't a frying pan will work too. As well as this, take out two saucepans and a mixing bowl.STEP 2Add thesri lankan daalsauce,red lentilsand 300ml of water (or 600ml for a 4 person box) to one of your saucepans and bring to a simmer, then cook for 10-15 mins until the lentils are soft and tender. Stir occasionally while cooking and add a splash of water as needed if it reduces too much or starts to stick. Season to taste with salt.STEP 3Add thebasmati riceto the other saucepan, cover with 2x its volume in water and place over a medium-high heat. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and cover, cooking for a further 8-10 minutes or until tender. Drain and set aside to keep warm.STEP 4Tear theoyster mushroomsinto 1.5-2cm strips and add them to your mixing bowl along with theoyster mushroom spice mix, a glug of neutral oiland a good amount of salt and pepper. Give everything a good mix with your hands until the mushrooms are well coated in marinade. Thread the marinated oyster mushroom pieces onto the wooden skewersSTEP 5Place your cast iron griddle or frying pan over medium-high heat and add a drizzle of oil to coat the bottom. Once the pan is searing hot, add the marinadedmushroom skewers. We recommend compressing the oyster mushrooms with a spatula or slotted turner so that they flatten and get crispier. Cook until crispy and blackened a little, around 5 minutes on each side.STEP 6In the meantime, drain and dice thecooked beetrootinto 1.5-2cm cubes and place in a mixing bowl. Add thelemon yoghurtand season generously with salt and pepper.STEP 7Divide the cookedbasmati ricebetween serving bowls and ladle over thesri lankan daal. Top with the crispyspiced oyster mushroom skewer, garnish with a few pickedcoriander leaves, and serve with a side ofbeetroot raita. Drain and set aside to keep warm.STEP 3Set your steamer over the hob and steam thekalefor 5-10 minutes or until tender.
